root_cause.summary: Hibernate ORM's CI run on OpenJDK 25 with hsqldb backend had a single test failure: `DbVersionTest.testCollectionVersion` at hibernate-core/src/test/java/.../version/db/DbVersionTest.java:60. The test asserts (`assertFalse`) that the owner entity's @Version field SHOULD NOT be incremented when its child collection is mutated (because the test verifies a behavior where updating a collection's contents alone shouldn't bump the parent version) — but on hsqldb the actual behavior bumped it: `owner version not incremented ==> expected: <false> but was: <true>`. The error trace shows the standard JUnit Jupiter assertion-failure stack (AssertionFailureBuilder.build → AssertFalse.failNotFalse → DbVersionTest.testCollectionVersion). One test failed out of thousands; Gradle test runner reports `> Task :hibernate-core:test FAILED` (L45955) → `FAILURE: Build failed with an exception.` (L45958) → `BUILD FAILED in 17m 39s` (L46120) → `##[error]Process completed with exit code 1` (L46121). Other backend variants (mysql, mariadb, etc.) ran in parallel matrix legs and have their own failures, but this case is scoped to the OpenJDK 25 hsqldb leg only.

required_signals.value: org.opentest4j.AssertionFailedError: owner version not incremented ==> expected: <false> but was: <true>
FAILURE: Build failed with an exception.

evidence_spans.reason: Self-contained failure block: `Gradle Test Executor 25 > DbVersionTest > testCollectionVersion(SessionFactoryScope) FAILED` + `AssertionFailedError: owner version not incremented ==> expected: <false> but was: <true>` + JUnit Jupiter assertion-stack ending at `DbVersionTest.testCollectionVersion(DbVersionTest.java:60)`. Names the test, the assertion shape, and the failing source line.

Gradle build summary: `> Task :hibernate-core:test FAILED` + `FAILURE: Build failed with an exception. * What went wrong: Execution failed for task ':hibernate-core:test'.` + GHA exit 1. Confirms the single test failure propagated through Gradle to the job exit code.

must_not_claim: the test source code is wrong
